title: Clan Fett
content:
{{Top|canon=Fetts}}

{{Quote|Beware the wrath of the Fetts!|[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]|<ref name="BF:FtS">''[[Boba Fett: The Fight to Survive]]''</ref>}}
'''Clan Fett''' was an ancient [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] clan that could trace its lineage back as far as the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Drawing its name from the [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] transliteration of the word ''vhett'', the [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']] word for [[Farmer/Legends|farmer]], members of the Fett clan throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] history ranged from simple farmers, to conquering [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]], in addition to several [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] throughout the [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], Clan Fett developed a reputation for members who were skilled and ruthless in battle, as well as for their efforts to uplift the Mandalorian people.

==History==
{{Quote|Funny how I've only really got to grips with my own messy family since I've been talking to a ''shabla'' Jedi.|[[Mirta Gev]]|<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>}}
An ancient [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] clan, the Fett clan had been in existence since the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Mandalorian Wars]],<ref name="Comics">[[Star Wars Insider 97|''Star Wars Insider'' 97]]</ref> thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> In proper [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]], the clan would be referred to as ''Vhett aliit''.<ref name="Vhett">According to [[Karen Traviss]]'s [http://karentraviss.com/page20/page26/page25/index.html grammar], the /f/ phoneme does not exist in [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]], which is why [[Arla Fett]] referred to herself as Vhett in ''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''. Also according to Traviss's [http://www.karentraviss.com/page20/page26/downloads/index.html lexicon], the Mando'a word for "clan" is ''aliit''.</ref> Perhaps the earliest member of the Fett clan known to history was [[Cassus Fett]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] Mandalorian who grew to infamy as [[Mandalore the Ultimate]]'s second-in-command in the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusader]] movement that sparked the Mandalorian Wars with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="KotORCG" /> The Neo-Crusaders' dreams of conquering the galaxy under Mandalorian rule were dashed, however, by the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], and their [[Army/Legends|armies]] [[Battle of Malachor V|defeated]] in battle at [[Malachor V]], ending the Mandalorian Wars.<ref name="NEC" />

[[File:Concorddawn.jpg|thumb|left|180px|[[Jaster Mereel]] becomes a surrogate second father to [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] after rescuing him.]]
Millennia later, following the foundation of the peaceful [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorian]] faction in the aftermath of the [[Mandalorian Excision]], Clan Fett found itself divided among the pacifist New Mandalorians and the [[Aka'liit|traditional warrior clans]].<ref name="TBHC">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]''</ref> By the year [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> a small family from the Fett clan were making an agricultural living for themselves as [[Farmer/Legends|farmers]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]].<ref name="OpenSeasons" /> Though Concord Dawn was located in the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> and had a long history of being affiliated with the Mandalorians,<ref name="TMPaC">{{InsiderCite|86|The Mandalorians: People and Culture}}</ref> the Fett family living on Concord Dawn were not Mandalorian.<ref name="O66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref> The eldest [[Fett (Elder Journeyman Protector)|Fett male]] was a [[Journeyman Protector/Legends|Journeyman Protector]], a [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|keeper of law]] on Concord Dawn; he was [[Marriage/Legends|married]], and was the [[Parent/Legends|father]] of two [[Youngling/Legends|children]], his son [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]],<ref name="OpenSeasons" /> and his daughter [[Arla Fett]].<ref name="O66" /> The family owned a [[Fett homestead|farm]] on the [[Agriworld/Legends|agricultural world]], in addition to a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] and a [[Harvester/Legends|crop harvester]] that was used to maintain the farm. Tragedy befell the Fett family when Concord Dawn became the scene of a [[Battle of Concord Dawn|battle]] between the forces of the violent Mandalorian splinter group known as the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]], and the loyalist [[True Mandalorians]] who opposed them,<ref name="OpenSeasons" /> each faction counting members of the Mandalorian Fett clan among their supporters.<ref name="TBHC" /> The Death Watch [[Secret Mandalore|leader]], [[Tor Vizsla]], [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] and later [[Death/Legends|killed]] the elder Fett for harboring the current [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] and leader of the True Mandalorians, [[Jaster Mereel]]; Fett's son Jango was rescued by Mereel and his [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], but [[Jango Fett's mother|his wife]] was later killed,<ref name="OpenSeasons" /> and his daughter Arla kidnapped by the Death Watch.<ref name="O66" /> Nevertheless, members of Clan Fett did serve not only in the Death Watch, but in the [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorian]] faction as well.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Under Jaster Mereel's fatherly tutelage, Jango Fett followed in the footsteps of his ancestors and became a Mandalorian.<ref name="Comics" /><ref name="OpenSeasons" /> Maturing into a skilled fighter, by the time Jango was fourteen years old<ref name="Blog-holocron|13/comments">{{Blog|holocron|13/comments|Major Character Birth Years}} states that Jango was born in [[66 BBY/Legends|66 BBY]], and the Battle of Korda 6 took place in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]] per ''Jango Fett: Open Seasons''</ref>—an adult in Mandalorian culture<ref name="TMPaC" />—he was actively fighting alongside Mereel. That would change in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]], when the Death Watch [[Battle of Korda Six|ambushed]] the True Mandalorian army on [[Korda Six]], and Vizsla killed Mereel during the battle. With Mereel dead, Jango ascended to the position of ''Mand'alor'' and led the Mandalorians throughout the remainder of the [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Mandalorian Civil War]] against the Death Watch up until the [[Battle of Galidraan]], where Tor Vizsla's gift for deceit brought a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] striketeam down on the True Mandalorians, under the mistaken belief that they had been slaughtering innocents. The True Mandalorians were defeated, and though Jango Fett killed six Jedi with his bare [[Limb/Legends|hands]], he was captured and turned over to [[Governor of Galidraan|the Governor]] of [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]], only to be subsequently [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|sold]] into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] by the [[Governor/Legends|governor]]. Slavery did not hold Jango for long, however, as he escaped during a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] raid on the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] where he was being held. Once free, he reclaimed his lost [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] from Galidraan's governor and hunted Vizsla to the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. There, Jango destroyed [[Death Rattle|Vizsla's ship]] and after a lengthy fight with Vizsla on the world's surface, left the Death Watch leader to die at the hands of a pack of [[dire-cat]]s.<ref name="OpenSeasons" /> With the war over, Jango turned to a life as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] to make a living.<ref name="OpenSeasons" /> In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Jango was recruited by [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] as the [[Clone template/Legends|progenitor]] for a [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]], after [[The Hunt|proving himself]] by killing [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Komari Vosa]].<ref name="BH">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''</ref> Entering into the service of Dooku, Jango acted as the Count's [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] during his time on the planet [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], and engaged several Jedi Knights on behalf of Dooku's newly formed [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]]. There in the [[Petranaki arena]], Fett faced off against [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], only to be [[Sai cha/Legends|slain]] by the veteran [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Councilor]].<ref name="AotC" />

[[File:Fett_happy_family.png|thumb|right|200px|[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] with his wife [[Sintas Vel]], and their daughter, [[Ailyn Vel|Ailyn]].]]
Jango's demise on Geonosis did not spell the end of the Fett [[Blood/Legends|bloodline]], for as part of his deal with Count Dooku to allow himself to be the clone army's progenitor, Fett demanded a clone of himself in return. This clone, which Fett named [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba]], was brought up as though he were Jango's own son. Boba Fett was raised on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]],<ref name="AotC" /> and received limited early training in the Mandalorian ways by his father before Jango's untimely death.<ref name="AotCVD">''[[Star Wars: Attack of the Clones: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> Following Jango's death, Boba claimed his [[Slave I/Legends|father's ship]] and [[Armor/Legends|armor]], and set out to make a living as a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter.<ref name="BF:FtS" /> When he was only sixteen, Boba met fellow bounty hunter [[Sintas Vel]]; the two fell in love, and were married, settling down together on Concord Dawn. The Fett clan grew with the [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] of a daughter, whom they named [[Ailyn Vel|Ailyn]]. Their happiness together was short lived: Sintas was raped by a [[Gender/Legends|man]] called [[Lenovar]], whom Fett killed upon learning of his crime. Exiled from Concord Dawn for the murder, Fett and Sintas split up and the two scarcely saw each other again. Ailyn grew to hate her absentee father as she got older, and attempted to kill him later in life.<ref name="Revelation" /> Under the rule of the Galactic Empire, Boba grew into a bounty hunter whose skill and fame even surpassed his father. He amassed a large fortune, taking jobs from the likes of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] and any who could pay. It was while working for Jabba that Fett was knocked into the [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] of a [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]] at the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], sustaining significant [[Acid/Legends|acid]] scarring that, although fading with age, would remain for the rest of his life.<ref name="Bloodlines">''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]''</ref> Fett escaped the sarlacc and continued his bounty hunting career for many years after, before eventually becoming ''Mand'alor'' in accordance with the last wish of a dying [[Fenn Shysa]], the previous ''Mand'alor''.<ref name="Revelation" /> Though he did not give up his bounty hunting career, Fett led the Mandalorians during the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of the galaxy,<ref name="BF:APM">''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]''</ref> and helped the planet [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] rebuild in the wake of the Vong's [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attack]] on the world.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

Though Boba had been gone from his daughter Ailyn's life since she was around two years old, life went on for Ailyn and her mother, Sintas. When Ailyn was sixteen years old, Sintas accepted a job in the hope of acquiring enough money to treat her daughter for her [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]]. However, Sintas never returned, having been captured and entombed in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]].<ref name="Revelation" /> With her mother now gone as well, Ailyn grew bitter and came to hate Boba Fett for leaving. She tracked her father for years, eventually killing a [[Spar|clone]] she believed to be him.<ref name="Bloodlines" /> Be that as it may, Ailyn's life was not without some respite from hate. At some point prior to the Yuuzhan Vong invasion, Ailyn met and fell in love with the Mandalorian warrior [[Makin Marec]]; the two were briefly married, a relationship that produced a daughter by the name of [[Mirta Gev|Mirta]]. Though the relationship did not last, Marec began training his daughter as a Mandalorian until he was suddenly killed during a [[Hull/Legends|hull]] breach in space.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

[[File:Mandalorian_wedding.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Boba Fett's granddaughter [[Mirta Gev]], and her husband, [[Ghes Orade]]]]
Ailyn raised Mirta to hate her grandfather, Boba Fett, and though it was Ailyn's vendetta that cost her her own life, it also served to reunite Fett with his granddaughter. Married once and subsequently divorced, Mirta—by that time using the surname Gev—attempted to reconcile with Fett after her mother's death.<ref name="Bloodlines" /> She grew closer to Fett as they spent more time together, and as facts about the past Ailyn hadn't known came to light. During this time, Mirta also became involved in a relationship with Mandalorian soldier [[Ghes Orade]], a relationship that brought the Fett clan closer to [[Clan Vevut]]. Mirta and Fett were often partners in Fett's endeavors to halt his terminal sickness,<ref name="Sacrifice" /> and later to recover Sintas Vel from carbonite. Emerging from carbonite after nearly thirty years, Sintas suffered from severe carbon sickness in the form of blindness and amnesia, and Mirta became determined to help her long-lost grandmother make a recovery. With the help of Jedi Knight [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]]—who was on Mandalore seeking the assistance of Fett—and the Jedi-turned-Mandalorian [[Bardan Jusik|Gotab]], Sintas was able to recover both her memories and her eyesight. Once restored to good health, Sintas was able to make peace with Boba Fett and start a relationship with her granddaughter, Mirta. It was also around this time that Mirta [[Wedding of Ghes Orade and Mirta Gev|married]] her lover, Ghes Orade. The marriage formed a connection between the Fett clan and the [[Clan Vevut|Vevut clan]] to which Orade belonged, and the reconciliation between Sintas, Boba, and Mirta marked the potential for a happier and more peaceful era for Clan Fett.<ref name="Revelation" />

By the time of the [[Sith–Imperial War]], the members of the Fett clan were renown for their skill and ruthlessness in battle. Clan Fett was also known for its cumulative efforts to benefit and uplift the Mandalorian people.<ref name="LegacyCG">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

It should be noted that while the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the Grand Army shared Jango Fett's [[DNA/Legends|DNA]], most felt no familiar connection with the man,<ref name="TZ">''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]''</ref> and aside from Boba, none were true members of the clan. The same held true for any of the clones' many offspring, including the likes of [[Venku Skirata]],<ref name="Revelation" /> the son of the Republic [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commando]] [[Darman Skirata|RC-1136]]—"Darman" and Jedi Knight [[Etain Tur-Mukan]],<ref name="O66" /> or [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC trooper]] [[Jangotat|Alpha-98's]] child with [[Sheeka Tull]].<ref name="The Cestus Deception">''[[The Cestus Deception]]''</ref>

==Notable members==
===Cassus Fett===
[[File:Cassusfetthead.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Cassus Fett]]
{{Main|Cassus Fett}}
{{Quote|You listen to Cassus Fett, and your son's sons will have nothing to do—because you'll have conquered everything!|Cassus Fett|<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 23">[[Knights of the Old Republic 23|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 23]]</ref>}}
The earliest known member of Clan Fett,<ref name="Comics" /> Cassus Fett was a Mandalorian human who lived during the time of the Mandalorian Wars. Second-in-command to Mandalore the Ultimate, Cassus spearheaded the rise of the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]], and used their more regimented structure and [[Neo-Crusader armor|standardized armor]] as the backbone of the Mandalorian forces during their war against the Republic.<ref name="KotORCG" /> Cassus Fett was responsible for the near extinction of the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] [[Species/Legends|race]],<ref name="NEC" /> as well as the [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|bombardment]] of both [[Serroco]]<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]''</ref> and [[Jebble]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Vector">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Vector]]''</ref> A master strategist and the victor of numerous battles, Cassus became one of the most wanted individuals in the galaxy during the war. Cassus was the father of at least [[Cassus Fett's son|one son]], and was to be presumed by most to be dead by the time of the [[Jedi Civil War]].<ref name="KotORCG" /><ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>

===Vorten Fett===
[[File:Vorten_Fett.png|thumb|right|150px|Vorten Fett]]
{{Main|Vorten Fett}}
{{Quote|Su cuy'gar, vod. What brings me the honor of having Mand'alor's chosen in my camp?|Vorten Fett|<ref name="Revenge" />}}
Centuries later, the Mandalorian commander Vorten Fett became a skilled commander and warrior within the Mandalorian clans. He led a thousand Mandalorian warriors during the [[Invasion of Taris]], which ultimately helped secure victories for their allies in the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. He was later present at a briefing on [[Ilum]] for a joint assault against Darth Malgus's [[New Empire]].

===Khomo Fett===
[[File:Khomo_Fett.png|thumb|right|150px|Khomo Fett]]
{{Main|Khomo Fett}}
{{Quote|"We'll break the siege of our homeworlds and smash Zakuul like in the days of Neo Crusaders!|Khomo Fett|<ref name="Revenge" />}}
Under the leadership of veteran Mandalorian warrior Khomo Fett, Clan Fett was forged into the most powerful clan within the [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]]. However, when the Mandalorians clashed with the [[Eternal Empire]], Clan Fett's zeal for battle cost them greatly as foolish mistakes killed many of the clan's warriors. Despite this, Khomo's name was put forward as an obvious choice for Mandalore's successor, though he refused the honor. Even though [[Mandalore the Avenger]] had concerns about his yearning for the glory days of the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]], his fierce loyalty to his people made him one of her top advisers and most trusted eyes and ears on the battlefield.

===Jango Fett===
{{Main|Jango Fett/Legends}}
[[File:JangoSansHelmet-SWE.png|thumb|right|200px|Jango Fett]]
{{Quote|Dad came from Concord Dawn. He said his family were frontier farmers. How did he get a Mandalorian name, then?|Boba Fett|<ref name="Bloodlines" />}}
The son of one of Concord Dawn's Journeyman Protectors, Jango Fett was born into the life of a farmer. When the Mandalorian Civil War, fought between the Death Watch and the True Mandalorians, spilled onto his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Jango's family was caught in the [[Blaster/Legends|crossfire]] and his parents were killed. Taken in by the leader of the Mandalorians, Jaster Mereel, Jango was subsequently adopted into the Mandalorian culture. After a Death Watch ambush on Korda 6 left Mereel dead, Jango took his surrogate father's place as ''Mand'alor'', leader of the Mandalorians, and led the True Mandalorian army until the Battle of Galidraan. There, Jango and the True Mandalorians fell victim to the machinations of the Death Watch, who tricked a Jedi striketeam into believing that Jango's soldiers had been slaughtering innocent civilians. Though Jango managed to personally kill six Jedi with his bare hands, the True Mandalorians were ultimately defeated and Jango arrested. Escaping from [[Slavery/Legends|captivity]], Jango tracked the Death Watch leader, Tor Vizsla, to Corellia and battled him to the death.<ref name="OpenSeasons" /> His loses and time in captivity made Jango a bitter man, and though he was still the ''Mand'alor'', he grew distant from the Mandalorian people,<ref name="O66" /> turning instead to a life as a bounty hunter. Chosen by Count Dooku as the progenitor of the clones of the Grand Army of the Republic,<ref name="OpenSeasons" /> Jango adopted an unmodified clone he named Boba as his son, viewing the child to be worth more to him than all the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] the job paid.<ref name="TZ" /> Though still a freelance bounty hunter,<ref name="Star Wars: Jango Fett (one-shot)">[[Star Wars: Jango Fett (one-shot)|''Star Wars: Jango Fett'']]</ref> Jango remained in the employ of Count Dooku and his Confederacy of Independent Systems up until his death during the Battle of Geonosis.<ref name="AotC" /> Jango's impressive skill and [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi killer]] reputation made him a symbol of strength for the Mandalorians.<ref name="O66" />

===Boba Fett===
{{Main|Boba Fett/Legends}}
[[File:HelmetlessFett.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Boba Fett]]
{{Quote|My dad was killed in front of me when I was thirteen. I was on the run for three years. I married Sintas at sixteen because I thought I could make my life right by doing what normal people did, but I was ''wrong''. I tried to be a Journeyman Protector but I killed a superior officer and I was jailed and exiled from Concord Dawn. And that was the end of trying to be a regular man. After that, I settled on being Boba Fett, because ''I just didn't know how to do anything else''.|Boba Fett|<ref name="Bloodlines" />}}
A clone of Jango Fett, Boba Fett was raised by Jango as his son. Growing up on Kamino,<ref name="AotC" /> Boba was only just beginning his Mandalorian training under Jango<ref name="AotCVD" /> before the elder Fett's untimely death, and thus didn't know much about the culture or how to speak the language.<ref name="Bloodlines" /> With his father dead, Boba took possession of Jango's ship and Mandalorian armor, determined to forge a life for himself as a bounty hunter.<ref name="BF:FtS" /> At the age of sixteen, he met and fell in love with the [[Kiffar/Legends|Kiffar]] bounty hunter Sintas Vel; the two fell in love and married, settling down together on Concord Dawn, where Fett took a job as a Journeyman Protector. Family life for Fett was short-lived: Sintas was raped by Fett's [[Lenovar|senior officer]]<ref name="Revelation" /> in the Protectors and Fett killed him in revenge, only to be jailed and exiled for his own crime. After that, Fett and Sintas split up and Fett focused on a renewed career as a bounty hunter, eventually earning a reputation as one of the best bounty hunters in the galaxy.<ref name="Bloodlines" /> Following the death of Fenn Shysa, Boba agreed to the man's dying wish to replace him as ''Mand'alor'',<ref name="Revelation" /> and led the Mandalorian people throughout the Yuuzhan Vong invasion of the galaxy, and found himself dedicated to the restoration of Mandalore following the war.<ref name="Sacrifice" /> It was also during this time that Fett met his estranged granddaughter, Mirta Gev; though raised by his daughter Ailyn to hate him, the death of Ailyn at the hands of the rising [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Caedus]] allowed Mirta and Boba to lay the groundwork for a new, closer relationship.<ref name="Bloodlines" /><ref name="Sacrifice" />

===Mirta Gev===
{{Main|Mirta Gev}}
[[File:Mirta_gev.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Mirta Gev]]
{{Quote|Mirta behaving herself? Well, at least she hasn't tried to kill you again. It's a good sign. Kids can be such a handful.''"<br />"''She's a useful pair of fists in a fight.''"<br />"''She'll produce wonderfully ferocious great-grandchildren for you, ''Bob'ika''.|Boba Fett and [[Goran Beviin]], discussing Mirta Gev|<ref name="Sacrifice" />}}
A Fett by [[Blood/Legends|bloodline]], Mirta Gev was the daughter of Ailyn Vel—Boba Fett's estranged daughter from his marriage to Sintas Vel—and the Mandalorian soldier, Makin Marec. Raised to hate Fett by her mother and trained to be a Mandalorian by her father, Mirta learned to be level-headed, tough, and resourceful from a young age.<ref name="Sacrifice" /> Mirta finally met her grandfather, Boba Fett, in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], when she played her part in her mother's latest plan to kill Fett. Attempting to lure Fett into a trap set by Ailyn, everything quickly fell apart when Ailyn was captured, interrogated, and accidentally killed by the up-and-coming [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Caedus.<ref name="Bloodlines" /> Their mutual grief over the loss of a loved one allowed Mirta and Fett to establish the foundation of a slow but steady relationship that grew over time, made stronger by the time they shared together while seeking a cure for Fett's late-in-life illness. Fett's appearance in Mirta's life also served to bring her deeper into the Mandalorian culture, and while staying on Mandalore, Mirta began to fall in love with the young Mandalorian soldier, Ghes Orade. During all of this, Mirta became interested in the fate of her vanished grandmother, and through her diligence, found that Sintas Vel was not dead, but in fact trapped within carbonite.<ref name="Sacrifice" /> Fett and Mirta rescued Sintas and released her from carbonite with the help of two of Fett's [[Clan Beviin|friends]]; when it became evident that Sintas had emerged suffering from severe carbonite sickness, Mirta dedicated herself to rehabilitating her long-lost grandmother. Aided in this endeavor by the [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]] abilities of the Jedi-turned-Mandalorian, Gotab, Mirta was able to assist Sintas in making a full recovery. With her grandmother healed, Mirta married Orade not long after, forming a bond between the Fett and Vevut clan to which Orade belonged.<ref name="Revelation" />

==Behind the scenes==
The roots of Clan Fett date back to the first appearance of Boba Fett in the [[1978]] animation, ''[[The Story of the Faithful Wookiee]]''. The revelation that Boba Fett was in fact the cloned son of Jango Fett in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], released twenty-four years later in [[2002]], established the Fetts as a family. This [[canon]]ical facet was later expanded upon with the ''[[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons|Jango Fett: Open Seasons'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, introducing Jango Fett's parents, and sister, Arla.

''www.ambufett.com'' was a domain name purchased by [[Lucasfilm]] before the release of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']]. It is possible that Ambu Fett may have been considered as the original name of Jango, or another Fett family member.<ref name="ESGH">{{Blog|arfmaul|21|Domain names: Ambu Fett, and.... Stiffy Borborygmus???}}</ref> Another name proposed for the character was J'mee Fett.<ref name="Jango Fett in the Databank">{{DB|character|jangofett|Fett, Jango}}</ref>

===Non-canon members===
====Melvin Fett====
{{Main|Melvin Fett}}
[[File:MFett.jpg|alt=|thumb|Melvin Fett]]
{{Quote|I've never actually successfully collected a bounty before. The other bounty hunters tease me quite a bit. Sometimes I cry.|Melvin Fett|<ref name="Melvin Fett">{{Tales|20|Melvin Fett (comic story)}}</ref>}}
Melvin Fett was a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who operated during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. He was the cousin of the renowned hunter [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], making his repeated failures at apprehending [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] even more comical to his peers. At some point before [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]], Fett accepted a bounty for the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in the hopes of redeeming himself in the [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] of his colleagues. Tracking Binks to the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] world [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Fett was caught unprepared by the planet's harsh climate, which put him at a tactical disadvantage. Such was his confusion that he mistook a [[Senator Jar Jar Binks commemorative drinking cup]] for the Senator himself.
